lobehub/lobe-chat#8760.body: ### 📦 部署环境 Vercel ### 📦 部署模式 服务端模式(lobe-chat-database 镜像) ### 📌 软件版本 v1.111.6 ### 💻 系统环境 Windows ### 🌐 浏览器 Chrome ### 🐛 问题描述 使用Google获取模型列表后，在设置页面能完整显示模型的扩展能力，但在对话页面的模型选择处，缺失联网搜索能力的图标 ### 📷 复现步骤 重新获取模型列表后出现该问题，`清除获取的模型`或`重置所有修改之后`恢复正常显示 ### 🚦 期望结果 _No response_ ### 📝 补充信息 _No response_
lobehub/lobe-chat#8760.title: Google获取模型列表后，对话页面模型选择处缺失联网搜索能力图标

lobehub/lobe-chat#9127.body:
### 📦 部署环境

Other

### 📦 部署模式

客户端模式(lobe-chat 镜像)

### 📌 软件版本

lobehub-desktop-beta 1.124.0

### 💻 系统环境

Ubuntu

### 🌐 浏览器

Other

### 🐛 问题描述

安装后，打开软件UI缺少最大化、最小化、关闭按钮，deb包和snap包效果相同，使用略有不便，建议修复。

### 📷 复现步骤

安装后，打开软件UI缺少最大化、最小化、关闭按钮，deb包和snap包效果相同

### 🚦 期望结果

以系统默认样式显示最大化、最小化、关闭按钮

### 📝 补充信息

_No response_
lobehub/lobe-chat#9127.title: On Ubuntu system, UI lacks maximize, minimize, close buttons

lobehub/lobe-chat#9501.body:
> [!NOTE]
> This issue was translated by Claude.

### 📦 Platform

Self hosting Docker

### 📦 Deploymenet mode

server db(lobe-chat-database image)

### 📌 Version

v1.133.1

### 💻 Operating System

Ubuntu

### 🌐 Browser

Edge

### 🐛 Bug Description

```json
{
  "code": "invalid_value",
  "type": "invalid_request_error",
  "error": {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "param": "input[4].content[0]",
    "message": "Invalid value: 'input_image'. Supported values are: 'output_text' and 'refusal'."
  },
  "param": "input[4].content[0]",
  "status": 400,
  "headers": {
    "content-type": "application/json",
    "content-length": "220"
  },
  "provider": "openai"
}
```

After this error occurred, I tried changing almost all settings, including switching API Keys, API proxy addresses, toggling the Responses API specification switch, switching client request modes, resetting the model list, configuring extended capabilities for models, etc. However, the error still persists.

This is my first time using the official OpenAI API, and this error appeared immediately. I did not change any settings before the error occurred - I was using the default settings.

In summary, no method of modifying application settings has been successful.

### 📷 Recurrence Steps

If and only if using the official OpenAI provider, the invalid_value error occurs regardless of which GPT model is used.

### 🚦 Expected Behavior

_No response_

### 📝 Additional Information

However, when using third-party relay APIs such as Openrouter's GPT models, I have not encountered any such issues.

---

<summary>Original Content</summary>

Self hosting Docker

### 📦 Deploymenet mode

server db(lobe-chat-database image)

### 📌 Version

v1.133.1

### 💻 Operating System

Ubuntu

### 🌐 Browser

Edge

### 🐛 Bug Description

```json
{
  "code": "invalid_value",
  "type": "invalid_request_error",
  "error": {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "param": "input[4].content[0]",
    "message": "Invalid value: 'input_image'. Supported values are: 'output_text' and 'refusal'."
  },
  "param": "input[4].content[0]",
  "status": 400,
  "headers": {
    "content-type": "application/json",
    "content-length": "220"
  },
  "provider": "openai"
}
```
出现该错误后,我尝试了更改几乎所有设置,包括换用 API Key,API 代理地址,Responses APl 规范开关,客户端请求模式切换,重置模型列表,针对模型配置扩展能力等等。但是错误仍然出现。
我是第一次使用 OpenAI 官方API,即出现该错误。我在出现错误前没有更改任何设置,即默认设置。
总而言之,没有任何通过修改应用设置的方法可以成功。

### 📷 Recurrence Steps

当且仅当使用OpenAI官方服务商时,无论使用任意GPT模型,都会出现invalid_value错误。

### 🚦 Expected Behavior

_No response_

### 📝 Additional Information

但是我在使用第三方中转API如 Openrouter 的GPT模型时,没有遇到任何此类问题。

</details>
lobehub/lobe-chat#9501.title: Invalid_value error occurs when calling OpenAI official provider API

lobehub/lobe-chat#9900.body:
### 📱 Client Type Web (Desktop Browser) ### 💻 Operating System Windows ### 📦 Deployment Platform Self hosting Docker ### 🔧 Deployment Mode server db (lobe-chat-database image) ### 📌 Version v1.141.6 ### 🌐 Browser Edge ### 🐛 Bug Description I input a document and asked GPT-5 to review it. The first response was normal. After the response, when I proceeded with follow-up questions, the output started including content from both the first question and the second follow-up question. I did not request in my second question to answer the first question again, but when I continued to ask questions, it still included the first question, or even only answered the first question. This problem has existed for a while. After disabling the response API, it returned to normal. Testing with CheeryStudio also worked normally. ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f862abfc-3a80-480f-965d-631e877d27c5) ### 📷 Recurrence Steps _No response_ ### 🚦 Expected Behavior _No response_ ### 📝 Additional Information The API uses a third-party proxy. I contacted the proxy service's customer support, and their internal testing was also normal. This issue only reproduces in LobeHub, and other models have no problems. ### 🛠️ Willing to Submit a PR? None ### ✅ Validations - [x] Read the [docs](https://lobehub.com/zh/docs). - [x] Check that there isn't [already an issue](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/issues) that reports the same bug to avoid creating a duplicate. - [x] Make sure this is a LobeChat issue and not a third-party library or provider issue. - [x] Check that this is a concrete bug. For Q&A, please use [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/discussions) or join our [Discord Server](https://discord.gg/rGHwKq4R). --- > This issue was translated by Claude.
Original Content ### 📱 Client Type Web (Desktop Browser) ### 💻 Operating System Windows ### 📦 Deployment Platform Self hosting Docker ### 🔧 Deployment Mode server db (lobe-chat-database image) ### 📌 Version v1.141.6 ### 🌐 Browser Edge ### 🐛 Bug Description 输入了一个文档,让gpt5进行检查,首次回复正常,回复后进行接下来的提问,开始输出包含了第一次问题+第二次后续提问的内容,我在第二次提问中并没有要求再回答第一次提问的问题,再接着提问还是会包含第一个问题,甚至只回答第一个问题。此前一直有这个问题,关闭response api后恢复正常,使用cheerystudio测试也正常。 ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f862abfc-3a80-480f-965d-631e877d27c5) ### 📷 Recurrence Steps _No response_ ### 🚦 Expected Behavior _No response_ ### 📝 Additional Information 其中api使用了第三方中转,联系中转站客服,他们内部测试也正常,只在lobehub会复现这个问题,其它模型无问题。 ### 🛠️ Willing to Submit a PR? None ### ✅ Validations - [x] Read the [docs](https://lobehub.com/zh/docs). - [x] Check that there isn't [already an issue](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/issues) that reports the same bug to avoid creating a duplicate. - [x] Make sure this is a LobeChat issue and not a third-party library or provider issue. - [x] Check that this is a concrete bug. For Q&A, please use [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/discussions) or join our [Discord Server](https://discord.gg/rGHwKq4R).
lobehub/lobe-chat#9900.title: When using OpenAI's response API, GPT-5 only answers the first question in a new conversation, and subsequent questions get irrelevant responses

lobehub/lobe-chat#10088.body:
### 📱 Client Type Web (Desktop Browser), Web (Mobile Browser), Desktop App (Electron) ### 💻 Operating System Windows, Android ### 📦 Deployment Platform Vercel ### 🔧 Deployment Mode server db (lobe-chat-database image) ### 📌 Version v2.0.0-next.33 ### 🌐 Browser Firefox ### 🐛 Bug Description When directly mentioning (@) a specific agent in an agent team, the conversation fails with an error. The behavior is that the conversation disappears directly. After refreshing the page on the web or restarting the desktop app, the user's sent message appears again without a response. Clicking retry shows an error: "Message send failed". This bug should have been introduced in recent versions after next.17 (there were also message display glitch issues before next.17). ### 📷 Recurrence Steps Create an agent team, @ mention one of them for conversation ### 🚦 Expected Behavior Normal response ### 📝 Additional Information \`\`\` { "shape": { "message": "Failed query: insert into \"messages\" (\"id\", \"role\", \"content\", \"reasoning\", \"search\", \"metadata\", \"model\", \"provider\", \"favorite\", \"error\", \"tools\", \"trace_id\", \"observation_id\", \"client_id\", \"user_id\", \"session_id\", \"topic_id\", lobehub/lobe-chat#10429.body:
### 📱 Client Type

Web (Desktop Browser)

### 💻 Operating System

Windows

### 📦 Deployment Platform

Self hosting Docker

### 🔧 Deployment Mode

server db (lobe-chat-database image)

### 📌 Version

v2.0.0-next.117

### 🌐 Browser

Chrome

### 🐛 Bug Description

The issue existed in lobechat 1.142.9 and persists after upgrading to lobehub. I found that the S3 file links from lobe have a problem. For example, when I download an image from the files section in lobe, the download link I get is https://s3.****.io/lobechat/files/490034/2919e339-422f-4aad-9132-ee82f0b94f65.jpeg. Opening this link directly redirects to Cloudflare's 404 page. However, when I find this image in Cloudflare's R2 bucket and get the download link, it is https://s3.****.io/files/490034/2919e339-422f-4aad-9132-ee82f0b94f65.jpeg - without "lobechat" in the middle. After thinking about it, the custom domain is bound to this bucket, so there indeed shouldn't be the bucket name in the middle.

My S3-related configuration is as follows:
- 'S3_ENDPOINT=https://******eee3e6042ddd749fd793581c.r2.cloudflarestorage.com' 
- 'S3_BUCKET=lobechat' 
- 'S3_PUBLIC_DOMAIN=https://s3.****.io' 
I tried removing S3_BUCKET=lobechat, but then the conversations and files failed to load.



### 📷 Recurrence Steps

_No response_

### 🚦 Expected Behavior

_No response_

### 📝 Additional Information

_No response_

### 🛠️ Willing to Submit a PR?

None

### ✅ Validations

- [x] Read the [docs](https://lobehub.com/zh/docs).
- [x] Check that there isn't [already an issue](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/issues) that reports the same bug to avoid creating a duplicate.
- [x] Make sure this is a LobeChat issue and not a third-party library or provider issue.
- [x] Check that this is a concrete bug. For Q&A, please use [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/discussions) or join our [Discord Server](https://discord.gg/rGHwKq4R).

---
> This issue was translated by Claude.

<summary>Original Content</summary>

Web (Desktop Browser)

Windows

Self hosting Docker

### 🔧 Deployment Mode

server db (lobe-chat-database image)

v2.0.0-next.117

### 🌐 Browser

Chrome

### 🐛 Bug Description

问题在lobechat1.142.9就存在,升级lobehub问题依旧。我发现是lobe这边的s3文件链接有问题,比如我在lobe这边文件找张图下载,获取的下载链接是https://s3.****.io/lobechat/files/490034/2919e339-422f-4aad-9132-ee82f0b94f65.jpeg  直接打开会调转cloudflare的404页面,而从cloudflare的R2存储桶里面找到这张图,获取的下载链接是https://s3.****.io/files/490034/2919e339-422f-4aad-9132-ee82f0b94f65.jpeg   没有中间的lobechat,我想了下,自定义域名是绑定到这个存储桶的,确实中间不应该有这个存储桶名字才对

### 📷 Recurrence Steps

_No response_

### 🚦 Expected Behavior

_No response_

### 📝 Additional Information

_No response_

None

### 📱 Client Type Web (Desktop Browser) ### 💻 Operating System macOS ### 📦 Deployment Platform Self hosting Docker ### 📌 Version v2.1.18 ### 🌐 Browser Chrome ### 🐛 Bug Description After upgrading to v2.1.18, the MoonShot Provider reports an error, all models are unavailable, and the chat interface prompts to enter the correct API Key (but the correct API Key has already been entered). In the Provider settings interface, the model test also fails. It's unclear why it's accessing Cloudflare. Complete error information: ```JSON { "error": { "status": 401, "headers": { "cf-cache-status": "DYNAMIC", "cf-ray": "9c8f8c1b4a512ab6-LAX", "connection": "keep-alive", "content-length": "84", "content-type": "application/json; charset=utf-8", "date": "Thu, 05 Feb 2026 04:08:33 GMT", "msh-request-id": "5697a4c6-0248-11f1-9d11-00163e7a3687", "msh-trace-mode": "on", "server": "cloudflare", "strict-transport-security": "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains", "vary": "Origin", "x-msh-trace-id": "cd6c577a593c854d91f9802cd255f9f2" }, "error": { "message": "Invalid Authentication", "type": "invalid_authentication_error" }, "type": "invalid_authentication_error" }, "endpoint": "https://api.moonshot.ai/v1", "provider": "moonshot" } ``` Self-hosting configuration is as follows: ```YAML # 月之暗面 Moonshot AI 开放平台 https://platform.moonshot.cn/ MOONSHOT_API_KEY=sk-xxxxxxxxxx MOONSHOT_MODEL_LIST=-all,+kimi-k2.5,+kimi-k2-0905-preview,+kimi-k2-thinking # OpenRouter AI https://openrouter.ai/ OPENROUTER_API_KEY=sk-or-v1-xxxxxxxxxxx OPENROUTER_PROXY_URL=https://gateway.ai.cloudflare.com/v1/xxxxxx/llm-gateway/openrouter OPENROUTER_MODEL_LIST=-all,+arcee-ai/trinity-large-preview:free ``` ### 📷 Recurrence Steps _No response_ ### 🚦 Expected Behavior MoonShot under KIMI should have worked normally in previous versions. I expect to be able to use models under MoonShot normally. ### 📝 Additional Information _No response_ ### 🛠️ Willing to Submit a PR? For Q&A, please use [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/discussions) or join our [Discord Server](https://discord.gg/rGHwKq4R). --- > This issue was translated by Claude.

lobehub/lobe-chat#12557.body:
# \[Bug] GLM Model Strips Tool Type Suffix Causing Tool Misidentification

## 📌 Issue Type

🐛 Bug

## 📱 Client Type

Web, Desktop App (Electron)

## 💻 Operating System

All

## 📦 Deployment Platform

All

## 📌 Version

Current canary branch

## 🌐 Browser

All

---

## 🐛 Bug Description

When using **GLM-4.7** (and potentially other models from Zhipu AI), the model returns tool names **without** the `____builtin` suffix, causing builtin tools to be misidentified as `default` type. This leads to tool execution failures for all builtin tools including Notebook, GTD, Memory, and WebBrowsing.

### Impact

- **All builtin tools fail** when called by GLM models
- Tools are incorrectly routed to the wrong executor (plugin executor instead of builtin executor)
- Users cannot use Notebook, GTD plans, Memory, or other builtin features with GLM models

---

## 📷 Recurrence Steps

1. Select GLM-4.7 (glm-4.7) as the model
2. Ask the AI to create a document using Notebook tool:
   ```
   Create a document titled "Hello World" with some content.
   ```
3. Observe the error in the browser console or network tab
4. Tool call fails because it's misidentified as `default` type instead of `builtin`

### Tool Call Comparison

**What GLM Returns** ❌:

```json
{
  "function": {
    "name": "lobe-notebook____createDocument", // Missing ____builtin suffix!
    "arguments": "{\"title\":\"Hello World\",\"content\":\"...\"}"
  },
  "id": "call_00_VQRIaPgZ1aneP84V0VQwmdtd"
}
```

**What DeepSeek Returns** ✅:

```json
{
  "function": {
    "name": "lobe-notebook____createDocument____builtin", // Correct full name
    "arguments": "{\"title\":\"Hello World\",\"content\":\"...\"}"
  },
  "id": "call_82ec3975c85f4481ad2f91cc"
}
```

**What the System Declares**:

```json
{
  "type": "function",
  "function": {
    "name": "lobe-notebook____createDocument____builtin",  // Full name in declaration
    "description": "Create a new document in the topic notebook...",
    "parameters": {...}
  }
}
```

---

## 🚦 Expected Behavior

1. **Models should return the complete tool name** as declared in the tool manifest
2. The system should **gracefully handle models that strip suffixes** with a compatibility layer
3. Tool type should be correctly identified as `builtin` regardless of model behavior
4. All builtin tools should work with GLM models just like they do with DeepSeek and GPT-4

---

## 🔍 Root Cause Analysis

### Location

`packages/context-engine/src/engine/tools/ToolNameResolver.ts:59-87`

### The Problem

1. System sends tool declarations with full name: `lobe-notebook____createDocument____builtin`
2. GLM returns: `lobe-notebook____createDocument` (strips the `____builtin` suffix)
3. Parser splits by `____` separator:
   ```typescript
   let [identifier, apiName, type] = toolCall.function.name.split(PLUGIN_SCHEMA_SEPARATOR);
   ```
4. Result: `["lobe-notebook", "createDocument", undefined]`
5. Since `type` is `undefined`, it defaults to `'default'`:
   ```typescript
   const payload: ChatToolPayload = {
     apiName,
     arguments: toolCall.function.arguments,
     id: toolCall.id,
     identifier,
     type: (type ?? 'default') as any, // ← Defaults to 'default'!
   };
   ```

### Why This Happens

GLM appears to "intelligently" strip the type suffix, considering it redundant. However, this violates the OpenAI Function Calling specification which requires models to return tool calls **exactly as declared**.

### Model Compliance Comparison

| Model       | Returns Full Name | Strips Suffix | Compliance       |
| ----------- | ----------------- | ------------- | ---------------- |
| DeepSeek    | ✅ Yes            | No            | ✅ Compliant     |
| GPT-4       | ✅ Yes            | No            | ✅ Compliant     |
| **GLM-4.7** | ❌ No             | **Yes**       | ❌ Non-compliant |

---

## 📝 Additional Information

### Tool Naming Convention

LobeHub uses the following naming convention for tools:

| Type       | Format                                    | Example                                           |
| ---------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| builtin    | `{identifier}____{apiName}____builtin`    | `lobe-notebook____createDocument____builtin`      |
| default    | `{identifier}____{apiName}`               | `custom-plugin____myAction`                       |
| standalone | `{identifier}____{apiName}____standalone` | `lobe-image-designer____text2image____standalone` |

### Why This Matters

The type suffix is critical for routing:

- **builtin** tools → executed by `BuiltinToolsExecutor`
- **default** tools → executed by `PluginGatewayService`
- **standalone** tools → executed as standalone agent tools

Wrong type = wrong executor = tool failure!

### Affected Tools

All builtin tools are affected when using GLM:

- ❌ Notebook (createDocument, updateDocument, getDocument, deleteDocument)
- ❌ GTD (createPlan, updatePlan, createTodos, updateTodos)
- ❌ Memory (addContextMemory, addUserActivity, etc.)
- ❌ WebBrowsing (search, crawlPage)
- ❌ Cloud Sandbox (all file and command operations)

### Related Specifications

According to [OpenAI Function Calling](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling) specification:

> The model should output tool calls that exactly match the tool definitions provided in the request.

GLM's behavior of modifying the tool name violates this specification.

---

## ✅ Validations

- [x] Read the [docs](https://lobehub.com/zh/docs).
- [x] Check that there isn't [already an issue](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/issues) that reports the same bug.
- [x] Make sure this is a LobeChat issue and not a third-party library or provider issue.
- [x] Check that this is a concrete bug with clear reproduction steps.

---

## 🔗 Related Issues

This is a model-specific issue that affects:

- All builtin tools
- Multi-agent workflows
- Users preferring GLM models

Consider also filing feedback with Zhipu AI about GLM's non-compliant behavior.

---
lobehub/lobe-chat#12557.title: [Bug] GLM Model Strips Tool Type Suffix Causing Tool Misidentification

lobehub/lobe-chat#12748.body:
### 🥰 Feature Description

### 1. Unnecessary `network-service` Container

**Current:** The deployment uses an alpine-based `network-service` container only for network namespace sharing.

**Issue:** This container is unnecessary with Docker's native bridge network and adds complexity.

**Proposed:** Remove `network-service` container and let all services use `lobe-network` bridge network directly.

---

### 2. Broken Documentation Link

**Current:** The "Custom Deployment" section links to `/en/docs/self-hosting/server-database` which returns 404.

**Location:** 
```markdown
<Callout type="info">
  This section does not cover all complete variables; remaining variables can be referenced in
  [Deploying with the Server Database](/en/docs/self-hosting/server-database).
</Callout>
```

**Proposed:**
Remove or update this link.

---

### 3. Unclear Port Mode vs Domain Mode

Current: The difference between Port Mode and Domain Mode is not clearly explained.

Proposed: Add clear explanation:

**Port Mode vs Domain Mode:**
- Port Mode: LobeHub automatically uses default ports (3210/9000/9001)
- Domain Mode: Configure custom ports via reverse proxy, then specify them in the setup script

---

### 4. Unclear RUSTFS_PORT Usage
**Current:** No guidance on when RustFS ports need to be exposed.
**Impact:** Users may:
- experience confusion when conversation image uploads fail while avatar uploads succeed.
- Submit bug reports thinking it's a malfunction (when it's expected behavior)
**Proposed:** Add clear documentation to:
- Set correct expectations upfront
- Reduce confusion and unnecessary bug reports

### 🧐 Proposed Solution

Pull Request is ready and can be submitted once maintainers approve this approach.


### 📝 Additional Information

_No response_
lobehub/lobe-chat#12748.title: [Request] Remove unnecessary network-service container and fix deployment docs

lobehub/lobe-chat#12784.body:
## Summary

- Added unit tests for `src/server/services/gateway`
- Total test files added: 2
- Test cases added: 37
- Coverage focus: `BotConnectQueue` Redis queue operations and `GatewayManager` lifecycle + bot management

## Changes

- [x] All tests pass successfully
- [x] Business logic coverage improved
- [x] Edge cases and error handling covered
- [x] Tests follow existing patterns

## Module Processed

`src/server/services/gateway`

## Test Coverage

- Functions tested:
  - `BotConnectQueue.push` — enqueueing with timestamp, Redis unavailable error
  - `BotConnectQueue.popAll` — parsing, expiry filtering, batch cleanup, malformed JSON, field format edge cases
  - `BotConnectQueue.remove` — field removal, Redis unavailable no-op
  - `GatewayManager.start` — lifecycle, idempotency, sync failure resilience
  - `GatewayManager.stop` — lifecycle, no-op when not running, stops all bots
  - `GatewayManager.startBot` — provider not found, unsupported platform, successful start, restart (stops existing)
  - `GatewayManager.stopBot` — bot not found, successful stop, isolation between bots
  - `createGatewayManager` / `getGatewayManager` — singleton pattern
- Coverage type: unit
- Test approach: mocked Redis, database, and KeyVaults dependencies; isolated state per test

---
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Summary by Sourcery

Add unit test coverage for the gateway service, focusing on gateway manager lifecycle and bot connect queue behavior.

Tests:
- Add GatewayManager unit tests covering lifecycle control, bot start/stop behavior, and singleton access helpers.
- Add BotConnectQueue unit tests covering Redis enqueue, dequeue, expiration handling, malformed data, and removal operations.
lobehub/lobe-chat#12784.title: ✅ test: add unit tests for gateway service

lobehub/lobe-chat#12817.body:
### 📱 Client Type
Desktop App (Electron)

### 💻 Operating System
All platforms

### 📌 Version
Current canary branch (as of 2026-03-08)

### 🐛 What happened?
React Error #185 occurs when clicking the model selection dropdown on the onboarding page (`/onboarding`).

**Error Message:**
```
Error: Minified React error #185; visit https://react.dev/errors/185 for the full message or use the non-minified dev environment for full errors and additional helpful warnings.

Matched leaf route at location "/" does not have an element or Component.
```

**Reproduction Steps:**
1. Navigate to `/onboarding` page
2. Click on the model selection dropdown button
3. Console shows React Error #185

### 🚦 What it should?
The model selection dropdown should open without errors, allowing users to select a model during onboarding.

### 📷 How to reproduce it?
See reproduction steps above.

### 📝 Additional Information
**Root Cause:**
This is a regression of #11905. The fix from that PR (`showInfoTag={false}`) was inadvertently reverted during PR #12587 (base-ui migration refactor).

**Timeline:**
- ✅ Jan 27, 2026: PR #11905 fixed this issue with `showInfoTag={false}`
- ❌ Feb 2026: PR #12587 refactor reverted the fix to `showInfoTag` (implicit true)
- ❌ Current: Error still occurs in production

**Technical Details:**
- **File:** `src/features/ModelSelect/index.tsx`
- **Line:** 131
- **Current Code:** `showInfoTag` (implicit true - causes error)
- **Fixed Code:** `showInfoTag={false}` (explicit false - prevents error)
lobehub/lobe-chat#12817.title: 🐛 Regression: Onboarding crash when clicking model selection dropdown (React Error #185)

lobehub/lobe-chat#12848.body: ### 📱 Client Type Web (Mobile Browser) ### 💻 Operating System Windows ### 📦 Deployment Platform Self hosting Docker ### 📌 Version 2.1.37 ### 🌐 Browser Chrome ### 🐛 What happened? **Regression:** Since the latest release, updated last night, version 2.1.37, whenever I create a new agent and make sure that absolutely no tools or skills whatsoever are selected, there's still 4655 Tokens used in the context as "Skill Settings". As you can see: - in the settings the agent config has no skills - in the chat there absolutely not skills activated (all buttons are greyed out and MCPs are all unchecked) - There's still tokens consumed in the "Skill Settings" - Certain tools are hard-coded as u can see in the last screenshot in which I asked the model which tools are available after making sure everything is disabled I used to have a custom agent, specifically because I didn't want to include all those skills by default like "LobeAI Agent" forced me to. ### 📷 How to reproduce it? 1. Create a new agent 2. Disable all skills 3. Open Chat 4. Disable all features, skills, and MCPs (web browsing, memory, GTD, Notbook, MCP) 5. Check how many tokens in the current context are consumed by "Skill Settings" 6. To confirm, that certain are hard-coded ask the model "what tools are currently available" ### 🚦 What it should be? The default context for new agents should be completely and 100% empty. ### 📝 Additional Information _No response_ ### 🛠️ Willing to Submit a PR? Yes, I am willing to submit a PR ### ✅ Validations - [x] Read the [docs](https://lobehub.com/zh/docs). - [x] Check that there isn't [already an issue](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/issues) that reports the same bug to avoid creating a duplicate. - [x] Make sure this is a LobeChat issue and not a third-party library or provider issue. - [x] Check that this is a concrete bug. For Q&A, please use [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/discussions) or join our [Discord Server](https://discord.gg/rGHwKq4R).
lobehub/lobe-chat#12848.title: Builtin skills are always enabled and can't be deactivated

## Summary by Sourcery

Reorganize settings and navigation, simplify default chat UI, and gate advanced controls behind a developer mode while updating terminology and suggestions across the app.

New Features:
- Add a consolidated Service Model settings page for agent, image, and speech configuration, and a new Appearance settings page combining general and chat appearance options.
- Introduce an agent profile header menu with advanced settings and community publish actions, including authentication, ownership checks, and publish result modals.
- Add rich default chat suggestion prompts and support a generic chat mode for suggested questions.
- Expose a notebook toggle in the conversation header actions and a dedicated Memory entry in the user panel menu.

Bug Fixes:
- Ensure suggested questions and prompts work correctly when no explicit mode is selected by defaulting to a chat mode.

Enhancements:
- Simplify the home experience by using a static welcome message, refining starter shortcuts, and hiding secondary content modules by default.
- Streamline the model switcher UI for regular users while keeping detailed views, grouping controls, and extra model info available only in developer mode, and adjust default panel sizing.
- Reduce visual complexity in chat by simplifying input actions, hiding token and usage metrics unless in developer mode, and making the send button more prominent for standard users.
- Revise settings information architecture and labels (e.g., General vs Agent groups, Appearance, Service Model, Credits, Usage) and add redirects from deprecated tabs.
- Unify footer and navigation entry points by moving GitHub, evaluation tools, and settings into contextual menus and simplifying bottom navigation.
- Simplify title tags in agent conversations to focus on topic titles instead of model and knowledge badges.
- Adjust language selection and user panel layouts to better integrate theme and locale controls.
lobehub/lobe-chat#12961.title: feat: Simplify UI

lobehub/lobe-chat#12971.body:
### 📱 Client Type

Web (Desktop Browser)

### 💻 Operating System

macOS

### 📦 Deployment Platform

Official Cloud

### 📌 Version

v2.1.40

### 🌐 Browser

Edge

### 🐛 What happened?

I set my xAI API key to use Grok 4.1 fast reasoning model and clicked the button to check. Got this error:

Model grok-4-1-fast-reasoning does not support parameter presencePenalty.

### 📷 How to reproduce it?

Go to providers, find xAI and set API Key, click check button.

### 🚦 What it should be?

It should work without any error.

### 📝 Additional Information

_No response_

### 🛠️ Willing to Submit a PR?

None

### ✅ Validations

- [x] Read the [docs](https://lobehub.com/zh/docs).
- [x] Check that there isn't [already an issue](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/issues) that reports the same bug to avoid creating a duplicate.
- [x] Make sure this is a LobeChat issue and not a third-party library or provider issue.
- [x] Check that this is a concrete bug. For Q&A, please use [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/discussions) or join our [Discord Server](https://discord.gg/rGHwKq4R).

lobehub/lobe-chat#12980.body:
### Client Type

Desktop App (Electron)

### Operating System

Windows

### Version

v2.1.42

### Browser

Chrome

### What happened?

Currently, the memory feature has two toggle points: one in system settings and one in Agent settings. Testing shows that when the system-level memory feature is enabled, even if the Agent-level memory feature is disabled, the Agent still invokes the memory feature; when the system-level memory feature is disabled, even if the Agent-level memory feature is enabled, the Agent still cannot invoke the memory feature. This causes confusion for users.

### How to reproduce it?

_No response_

### What it should be?

- When the system-level memory feature is enabled and the Agent-level memory feature is enabled, the Agent can invoke the memory feature;
- When the system-level memory feature is enabled and the Agent-level memory feature is disabled, the Agent cannot invoke the memory feature;
- When the system-level memory feature is disabled, the Agent-level memory feature defaults to disabled and the Agent cannot invoke the memory feature; if the user manually enables the memory feature in Agent settings, a prompt should appear indicating that the system-level memory feature is currently disabled, asking the user to confirm whether to also enable the system-level memory feature.

### Additional Information

_No response_

### Willing to Submit a PR?

None

### Validations

- [x] Read the docs.
- [x] Check that there is not already an issue that reports the same bug to avoid creating a duplicate.
- [x] Make sure this is a LobeChat issue and not a third-party library or provider issue.
- [x] Check that this is a concrete bug. For Q&A, please use GitHub Discussions or join our Discord Server.

---
> This issue was translated by Claude.

<details>
<summary>Original Content</summary>

目前记忆功能的开关有两处：系统设置中的和Agent设置中的。经测试，当系统设置中的记忆功能开启时，即使Agent设置中的记忆功能处于关闭状态，Agent依然会调用记忆功能；当系统设置中的记忆功能关闭时，即使Agent设置中的记忆功能处于开启状态，Agent依然不能调用记忆功能。这给用户带来混乱。

当系统设置中的记忆功能开启时，如果Agent设置中的记忆功能处于开启状态，则Agent能调用记忆功能；
当系统设置中的记忆功能开启时，如果Agent设置中的记忆功能处于关闭状态，则Agent不能调用记忆功能；
当系统设置中的记忆功能关闭时，Agent设置中的记忆功能默认处于关闭状态，Agent不能调用记忆功能；若用户手动在Agent设置中开启记忆功能，则弹出提示，说明目前系统设置中的记忆功能处于关闭状态，让用户确认是否开启系统设置中的记忆功能

</details>
lobehub/lobe-chat#12980.title: Memory Feature Toggle Hierarchy Confusion

lobehub/lobe-chat#13052.body: #### 💻 Change Type - [ ] ✨ feat - [ ] 🐛 fix - [ ] ♻️ refactor - [X] 💄 style - [ ] 👷 build - [ ] ⚡️ perf - [ ] ✅ test - [ ] 📝 docs - [ ] 🔨 chore #### 🔗 Related Issue #### 🔀 Description of Change 1. 增加多个视频生成模型（含文生视频，图生视频，首尾帧图生视频）的支持，生成结果如下 `zhipu` `minimax` `xai` `wenxin` `qwen` `siliconcloud` `openai (sora)` `google (veo)` 2. 加入 `handlePollVideoStatus` 用于后台获取生成状态并返回 `videoUrl` 3. 为 `downloadVideo` 加入 options.header 用于 OpenAI Sora / Google Veo 格式需要身份验证 Header 进行下载 --- 已知问题： 1. ~~轮询时任务提交界面不跳转，但任务已提交，保持界面不动时会在一段时间后重新提交第二遍（刷新页面后正常）~~ 已修复 2. ~~OpenAI Sora 与当前下载流程不匹配，需要传入 API Key 校验，待后续另起新 PR~~ 已支持 --- |Provider|Screenshot| |-|-| |zhipu|| |minimax|| |xai|| |siliconcloud|| |wenxin|| |qwen|| |oepnai/aihubmix|| |google|| #### 🧪 How to Test - [ ] Tested locally - [ ] Added/updated tests - [ ] No tests needed #### 📸 Screenshots / Videos | Before | After | | ------ | ----- | | ... | ... | #### 📝 Additional Information ## Summary by Sourcery Add synchronous video generation support for additional providers and update video duration handling across the stack. New Features: - Introduce Zhipu, MiniMax, Volcengine, and XAI video model cards to the model bank, including metadata, parameters, and pricing. - Support video generation for MiniMax, Zhipu, and XAI providers via new polling-based runtime implementations that return final video URLs. - Allow video creation APIs to optionally return an immediate video URL alongside the inference ID and process polling-based provider results without webhooks. Enhancements: - Extend the unified video parameter schema to support enum-based durations and update the UI to render segmented duration selectors when discrete options are available. - Adjust the video webhook handler initialization and logging to better support provider-specific behavior and placeholder API keys. - Improve async task tracking for video generation by recording task creation time and computing duration for polling-based flows.
lobehub/lobe-chat#13052.title: 💄 style: add more videoGen provider support

lobehub/lobe-chat#13071.body:
## Client Type
- Web (Desktop Browser)

## Operating System
- macOS

## Deployment Platform
- Official Cloud (reported through the `lobehub` provider route)
- Likely reproducible in any deployment that uses forked agents plus a strict OpenAI-compatible Responses API provider

## Version
- `2.1.42`
- `canary` HEAD `f8d51bbf4f`

## Browser
- Other (not confirmed from the payload)

## What happened?
Chatting with a forked agent on `gpt-5.4` through AiHubMix fails with:

- `Unknown parameter: 'forkedFromIdentifier'. (tid: 2026031705252643416489557845218)`
- provider error type: `ProviderBizError`

After tracing the request path, `forkedFromIdentifier` appears to be stored in `agent.config.params`, later spread into the LLM chat payload, and then forwarded as a top-level field to `client.responses.create(...)`. AiHubMix rejects it as an unknown parameter.

## How to reproduce it?
1. Open a published market agent and click `Fork & Chat`.
2. The fork flow stores `forkedFromIdentifier` under the new forked agent `config.params`.
3. Use `aihubmix` as provider and pick a Responses API model such as `gpt-5.4`.
4. Send a message to the forked agent.
5. The request fails with `Unknown parameter: 'forkedFromIdentifier'`.

## What it should be?
Business metadata such as `forkedFromIdentifier` should never be forwarded to model providers. Forked agents should chat normally on strict OpenAI-compatible providers and Responses API models.

## Additional Information
Suspected request path:

- `src/routes/(main)/community/(detail)/agent/features/Sidebar/ActionButton/ForkAndChat.tsx` writes `forkedFromIdentifier` into `config.params`.
- `src/store/chat/agents/createAgentExecutors.ts` spreads `...agentConfigData.params` into chat request params.
- `packages/model-runtime/src/core/openaiCompatibleFactory/index.ts` forwards remaining payload fields into `responses.create(...)`.
- The group fork path stores the same field under group `config`, so it does not leak in the same way.

This likely affects not only AiHubMix `gpt-5.4`, but any strict provider or model on the Responses API path that rejects unknown top-level params.

Possible fixes:

- Hotfix: strip non-LLM fields like `forkedFromIdentifier` before constructing chat and runtime payload.
- Proper fix: move fork metadata out of `agent.params` (which is typed as `LLMParams`) into dedicated metadata or config storage.

cc @arvinxx because this looks like a runtime request-shape bug rather than a provider-side outage.

## Willing to Submit a PR?
- No, but I am happy to help test the fix.
lobehub/lobe-chat#13071.title: Forked agents leak forkedFromIdentifier into Responses API payload and break AiHubMix gpt-5.4

lobehub/lobe-chat#13360.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview 联网/提取网页链接内容功能和技能/MCP 功能有冲突

lobehub/lobe-chat#13476.body:
## Bug Description

PR #13468 (`453db9f`) added request context enrichment (provider, model, elapsedMs, networkStatus) to fetch-sse error handling. However, the contextBody is injected into **all** errors unconditionally, including structured provider errors (e.g. `ProviderBizError`) that already have their own complete `body`.

### Current behavior

For errors with `error.type` (structured errors from the server), the code does:

```ts
{ ...error, body: { ...error.body, ...contextBody } }
```

This means `contextBody` fields like `provider`, `model` are spread into the existing error body, **overwriting** or **polluting** the original error structure.

### Example

A `ProviderBizError` like this:
```json
{
  "error": {
    "error": {
      "type": "invalid_request_error",
      "message": "messages.1.content.0: Invalid signature in thinking block"
    },
    "provider": "lobehub",
    "errorType": "ProviderBizError"
  },
  "model": "claude-opus-4-6",
  "provider": "lobehub",
  "errorType": "ProviderBizError"
}
```

Gets its body contaminated with `elapsedMs`, `networkStatus`, and potentially has `provider` overwritten by the request context value.

### Expected behavior

Context enrichment should **only** apply to unstructured/unknown errors (the `else` branch where `!error.type`). Structured provider errors already contain all the context they need and should be passed through unchanged.

### Root cause

`packages/fetch-sse/src/fetchSSE.ts` line 326:
```ts
error.type
  ? { ...error, body: { ...error.body, ...contextBody } }  // ← pollutes structured errors
  : { ... }
```

### Fix

For the `error.type` branch, pass the error through without modifying its body, or put contextBody under a separate non-conflicting key (e.g. `_requestContext`).

### Related PRs

- #13468 — introduced the contextBody injection
- #13469 — partially fixed field rename but didn't address body pollution
lobehub/lobe-chat#13476.title: 🐛 fetch-sse contextBody injection overwrites structured provider errors
